name: CRLF Injection
description: CRLF injection vulnerability exists if an attacker can inject the CRLF characters into a web application, for example using a user input form or an HTTP request.

name: Server Side Request Forgery
description: A Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) attack can often result in unauthorized actions or access to data within the organization, either in the vulnerable application itself or on other back-end systems that the application can communicate with.

name: Default Credentials Used
description: A default password is a password (usually '123', 'admin', 'root', 'password', or 'access') assigned to a program or hardware device by the developer or manufacturer.

name: Unauthorized Access
description: Access to others' user data or manipulation of the other user using POST data parameter tampering.

name: Privilege Escalation
description: Any user can gain admin access by accessing admin URL or manipulating the request/response.

name: Parameter Manipulation
description: Attacker can manipulate parameters to access any user data and it can lead to further attacks.

name: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
description: Attacker, who is an authorized system user, simply changes a parameter value that directly refers to a system object to another object the user is not authorized for. Such flaws can compromise all the data that can be referenced by the parameter.

name: Security Misconfiguration
description: Attackers will often attempt to exploit the risky open Ports to gain unauthorized access or knowledge of the system.

name: Brute Force Attack
description: Brute force attacks are simple and reliable. Attackers let a computer do the work — trying different combinations of usernames and passwords.

name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Cross-site scripting vulnerabilities arise when user input is stored and later embedded into the application's responses in an unsafe way. An attacker can use the vulnerability to inject malicious JavaScript code into the application.
remediation: Sanitize user input by implementing a library like DOMPurify on the client-side and appropriate encoding on the server-side.

name: HTML Injection
description: HTML injection attack is discovered in your web application. When an application does not properly handle user supplied data, an attacker can supply valid HTML code and inject their own content into the page.

name: Host Header Injection
description: Developers are trusting the HTTP Host header value and using it to generate links, import scripts and even generate password resets links with its value. This is a bad idea because the HTTP Host header can be controlled by an attacker.

name: Insecure Data Transmission
description: Information transmitted over an unencrypted channel. This information should always be transferred via an encrypted channel (HTTPS) to avoid being intercepted by malicious users.

name: Blind SQL Injection
description: It is possible to gain access to the database by injecting SQL commands into data-plane input in order to affect the execution of predefined SQL commands.

name: Unvalidated URL Redirect
description: It is possible to redirect a user to an untrusted site when the user visits a link located on a trusted website.

name: Authentication Bypass
description: It was identified that all users have the same privilege with admin rights.

name: Authorization Bypass
description: It was identified that all users have the same privilege with admin rights.

name: OTP Bypass
description: OTP Verification can be bypassed using Virtual Free Numbers. There are various apps/tools which provide Virtual Free Numbers using which you can bypass OTP verification.

name: Weak Session Management
description: Since HTTP is a stateless protocol, session management capabilities are required to link authentication and access control modules. Weak session management can allow session hijacking.

name: Session Fixation
description: The application does not renew its session cookie(s) after a successful user authentication, enabling session hijacking and infiltration attacks.

name: SQL Query Disclosure
description: This page contains one or more comments that look like SQL statements. These SQL statements may disclose sensitive information to an attacker.

name: Email Addresses Disclosed
description: Majority of spam comes from email addresses harvested off the internet. Spambot programs look for strings like myname@mydomain.com and then record any addresses found.

name: Database Connection String Disclosed
description: A database connection string specifies information about a data source and the means of connecting to it. Exposing this in web applications is a security risk.

name: Auto-Complete Feature Enabled
description: An attacker with local access could obtain the cleartext password from the browser cache, if the username is stored as part of autocomplete functionality.

name: Insecure Frame (External)
description: IFrame sandboxing enables a set of additional restrictions for the content within a frame. Without this, malicious iframes can cause harm to the web page that embeds them.

name: Default Server Page Disclosure
description: It was identified that an IP with port 8080 is redirected to Web Server Sample document pages.

name: Reverse Tabnabbing
description: Open windows with hrefs using the tag target='_blank' can modify window.opener.location and replace the parent webpage with something else, even on a different origin.

name: X-Powered-By Header Disclosure
description: Revealing server [x-powered-by: Asp.net] information might enable the attacker to exploit vulnerabilities associated with the corresponding application type.

name: Test Page Detected
description: Testing-related application page was found. Test pages usually do not adhere to the security requirements/guidelines of the rest of the application, making them a potential security hazard.

name: X-AspNet-Version Header Disclosure
description: The value of this header discloses the version of ASP.NET in use. It is not necessary for production sites and should be disabled.

name: Server Banner Disclosure
description: The Webserver details are disclosed which will help the attackers to narrow down the scope of attack. It is advisable not to disclose such information.

name: Directory Listing
description: The web server is configured to display the list of files contained in this directory. This allows a user to view a list of all files, possibly exposing sensitive information.

name: Weak Lockout Mechanism
description: Account lockout mechanisms are used to mitigate brute force password guessing attacks. Accounts are typically locked after 3 to 5 unsuccessful login attempts.

name: Insecure TLS Version Enabled
description: An attacker may be able to exploit this problem to conduct man-in-the-middle attacks and decrypt communications between the affected service and clients.

name: Remove Weak Ciphers in TLS 1.2
description: An attacker may be able to exploit this problem to conduct man-in-the-middle attacks and decrypt communications between the affected service and clients.

name: Invalid SSL Certificate
description: An SSL certificate can be created and signed by anyone. If you have an invalid certificate, your visitors may have trouble distinguishing between your certificate and those of attackers.

name: Session Token in URL
description: Application is forwarding Session token in URL. Sensitive information within URLs may be logged in various locations, including the user's browser and the web server.

name: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Misconfiguration
description: If a website responds with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * the requested resource allows sharing with every origin. Therefore, any website can make XHR requests to your site and access the responses.

name: Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF)
description: Cross-site request forgery is a type of malicious exploit of a website whereby unauthorized commands are transmitted from a user that the website trusts.

name: Directory Indexing
description: Depending on the operating system and distribution, the default configuration of Apache allows the indexing of directories, enabling search engines to index your file structure and possibly revealing sensitive data.

name: Insecure HTTP Methods Enabled
description: HTTP OPTIONS method is enabled on this web server. The OPTIONS method provides a list of methods that are supported by the web server.

name: Missing Strict-Transport-Security Header
description: HTTP strict transport security (HSTS) enforces the connections in HTTPS disregarding any script's call to load any resource in that domain over HTTP.

name: Change Password Feature Missing
description: In the application there is no change password feature and any user can change password for any users without admin rights.

name: Sensitive Information in URL
description: Information exposure through query strings in URL is when sensitive data is passed to parameters in the URL. This allows attackers to obtain sensitive data such as usernames and passwords.

name: Unprotected File
description: Insecure deployment and exposure of files can supply attackers with invaluable information about the application enabling them to orchestrate targeted attacks.

name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
description: It is possible to view server documentation, server information and server sample pages in the browser.

name: CSV Injection
description: It occurs when the data in the file is not properly validated prior to export. The attacker usually injects a malicious payload or formula into the input field.

name: Weak Encoding
description: It was identified that Base64 encoding is used to encode the data. Encoding is not encryption and provides no security.

name: Anti-Automation
description: It was identified that attacker can perform form based brute force or dictionary attack to compromise the application.

name: Complex Password Policy Missing
description: It was identified that the application does not follow the password policy and accepts guessable words (dictionary words).

name: Sensitive Information Disclosure in Cookie
description: It was identified that user information and IP address are stored in the Cookie.

name: Concurrent Login Enabled
description: It was identified that the same session is being used by multiple users at the same time (Multi Login enabled).

name: Weak Password Change/Reset Functionality
description: It was noticed that while changing the Password it does not ask for the Old Password, so any user can change password without an old password.

name: CCS Injection (SSL/TLS MITM Vulnerability)
description: OpenSSL's ChangeCipherSpec processing has a serious vulnerability allowing malicious intermediate nodes to intercept encrypted data and decrypt them while forcing SSL clients to use weak keys.

name: Improper Error Handling
description: Potential vulnerability due to the presence of a fully qualified path name in an error message. This information will help the attacker to understand about the application to enumerate further.

name: Unrestricted URL Access
description: Restrict access to sensitive URLs. Unauthorized users should not be able to access admin or restricted pages directly.

name: Missing Secure Cookie Attributes
description: Secure and http-only attributes need to be added to cookie flags of all pages to prevent cookie theft.

name: Encode Used Instead of Encryption
description: Sensitive information about passwords are encoded and stored. Encoding (e.g. Base64) is not encryption and provides no security.

name: Missing X-Content-Type-Options
description: Setting this header will prevent the browser from MIME-sniffing a response away from the declared content-type.

name: Session Not Terminated After Logout
description: The application does not invalidate current session cookie(s) after a user logout. An expired cookie can be used to authenticate a malicious user.

name: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
description: The web server did not return an X-Frame-Options header which means that this website could be at risk of a clickjacking attack.

name: Missing Content Security Policy Header
description: This response header allows web site administrators to control resources the user agent is allowed to load for a given page. This helps guard against cross-site scripting attacks.

name: Filepath Disclosure
description: This vulnerability reports pages on which bad input can lead to an internal application error which reveals the path information.

name: Browser Cache Weakness
description: Unless directed otherwise, browsers may store a local cached copy of content received from web servers. If sensitive information is stored in the local cache, it may be retrieved by other users.

name: User Enumeration
description: User enumeration is when a malicious actor can use brute-force techniques to either guess or confirm valid users in a system.

name: Source Code Disclosure
description: Using a source code disclosure attack, an attacker can retrieve the source code of server-side scripts such as ASP, PHP and JSP, granting deeper knowledge of the application logic.

name: Unrestricted File Upload
description: Various web applications allow users to upload files. If not handled correctly, a remote attacker could upload a file of dangerous type that can be automatically processed within the product's environment.

name: Using Components with Known Vulnerabilities
description: Vulnerable versions of third-party components (e.g. jQuery, Bootstrap) have been used. These components may contain known security issues.

name: HTTP Verb Tampering
description: GET and POST methods may be interchangeable, allowing attackers to bypass access controls or perform unintended state-changing operations.

name: Missing XSS Protection Header
description: X-XSS-protection header helps in enabling the cross-site scripting (XSS) filter built into browsers.

name: DEBUG Enabled
description: Django web application is running with debug mode turned on (DEBUG = True). This displays a detailed traceback including environment metadata when an exception occurs.

name: Implement Session Timeout Policy
description: Session timeout represents the event occurring when a user does not perform any action on a web site during a defined interval. Without timeout, sessions remain active indefinitely.

name: Content Spoofing
description: Content spoofing is an attack where an attacker can supply content to a web application, typically via a parameter value, that is reflected back to the user under the context of the trusted domain.

name: BREACH Attack Detected
description: Weak nonce value is used in the HTML attributes, making the application susceptible to the BREACH compression attack.

name: Session Hijacking
description: Session Hijacking attack compromises the session token by stealing or predicting a valid session token to gain unauthorized access to the Web Server.

name: Duplicate Headers
description: Duplicate headers are present in the response, which can lead to inconsistent interpretation by clients and security controls.

name: CSP-Unsafe Directives Used
description: It was identified that unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval directives are used in the Content Security Policy header.

name: Unvalidated Input
description: It was identified that special characters and malicious JavaScript payloads are accepted in the input field with no restrictions.

name: Sensitive Data in Local Storage
description: An attacker can gather sensitive information based on the data stored locally in the browser's local storage.

name: Missing Character Length Validation
description: An attacker may flood the input field with a huge amount of characters, thereby crashing the server.

name: Broken Functionality
description: Part of the application was not functional during the testing period.

name: Broken Access Control
description: Broken access control is a type of vulnerability that allows unauthorized users to gain access to sensitive data or systems due to weaknesses in authentication and authorization enforcement.

name: Parameter Tampering
description: Attacker can manipulate parameters to access any user data and it can lead to further attacks.

name: Missing Character Limit Validation
description: It has been identified that the character limit has not been set in the input field.

name: CSP Header Misconfiguration
description: Unsafe directives have been found in the Content Security Policy Header which can allow the execution of unsafe scripts.

name: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
description: It has been identified that the API rate limit for the process has not been set, allowing potential abuse.

name: Business Logic Error
description: A malicious user may modify system data in a way that disrupts the business process or logic.

name: Broken Object Level Authorization
description: Unauthorized access to other users' objects can result in data disclosure, data loss, or data manipulation. Under certain circumstances, this can also lead to full account takeover.

name: Insufficient Session Expiration
description: Insufficient session expiration allows attackers to reuse old session credentials or IDs, potentially gaining unauthorized access and escalating security risks.

name: Mass Assignment
description: Mass assignment occurs when an API automatically binds client-provided data to internal objects without proper filtering, allowing attackers to modify sensitive properties they should not have access to.

name: No Vulnerability Found
description: No active vulnerabilities have been identified during this assessment.
